# UC-19.1.46 · OpenStack Galera / MariaDB Cluster Non-Primary or Split-Brain Indicators

## Description

The OpenStack SQL database underpins Keystone, Nova, Neutron, and Cinder metadata. A Galera node that is not `Primary` or shows partition comments risks write outages and API 500s across the cloud.

## Value

Stops multi-service OpenStack brownouts by detecting Galera cluster role loss before API error storms peak.

## Implementation

Run `mysql -e "SHOW STATUS LIKE 'wsrep%';"` on a schedule and HEC JSON, or parse error log lines. Tag events with `openstack_db` index. Alert immediately when any node reports non-Primary. Require two-source confirmation before automation to avoid flapping.

## SPL

```spl
index=openstack (sourcetype="mysql:galera" OR sourcetype=syslog) earliest=-30m
| search wsrep OR "Galera" OR "non-Primary" OR "Non-primary"
| eval cluster_ok=coalesce(wsrep_cluster_status, cluster_status)
| where cluster_ok!="Primary" OR like(lower(_raw), "%non-primary%") OR like(lower(_raw), "%partition%")
| stats latest(cluster_ok) as status, values(wsrep_local_state_comment) as state by host
| sort status
```

## Visualization

Table (node cluster state), Single value (non-primary count), Timeline (wsrep transitions).

## References

- [Galera Cluster — Monitoring cluster status](https://galeracluster.com/library/documentation/monitoring-cluster.html)
